description_long: This object is a vintage portable radio receiver with a rectangular body and a faux woodgrain finish on the top and sides. The front face features a cassette tape deck with a transparent window, a set of control buttons, and a large speaker grille with horizontal slats. Two prominent black dials are located on the top surface, likely for tuning and volume control, alongside several push buttons and switches. A sturdy black handle arches over the top, allowing for easy carrying. The side panels are simple, with one side showing two small circular ports or jacks. The overall design is reminiscent of classic 1980s or 1990s boomboxes, combining both radio and cassette playback functionality.
description: A vintage portable radio receiver with a woodgrain finish, cassette deck, large speaker grille, and black carrying handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cassette deck, control buttons, speaker grille, and part of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the woodgrain panel and two small circular ports or jacks.
description_view_2: Rear/top view displaying the two large dials, control buttons, and the back panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a woodgrain panel and two small circular ports or jacks.
